Simhasanam-1986

# Simhasanam-1986

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![r/tollywood - Simhasanam was the Bahubali of it's time, the first ever Telugu movie to be shot in 70 mm, 6 track stereophonic sound this 1986 period epic folklore movie, was a sensation in many ways.](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!9O0C!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F2a9daeeb-aa6f-4a6f-8a98-5286d1ccc3d5_270x369.png "r/tollywood - Simhasanam was the Bahubali of it's time, the first ever Telugu movie to be shot in 70 mm, 6 track stereophonic sound  this 1986 period epic folklore movie, was a sensation in many ways.")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

*Simhasanam* was a landmark in Telugu cinema. Directed by Krishna, it was the **first Telugu film shot in 70mm with 6-track stereophonic sound**, a technical marvel for its time. Much like *Baahubali* decades later, it was envisioned as a grand spectacle,an epic folklore drama blending palace intrigue, mythology, and larger-than-life visuals.

While the 70mm format and stereophonic sound gave audiences an immersive experience unprecedented in Telugu cinema, the **l**avish sets, battle sequences, and mythological motifs made it feel like a cinematic festival. Krishna not only directed but also starred in the film, cementing his reputation as a pioneer willing to push boundaries. It set a benchmark for how Telugu filmmakers could dream big, paving the way for later epics like *Baahubali*.

**Plot**

Simhasanam drew upon the stories of earlier Janapada movies in Telugu, with a story that had all the elements-intrigue, betrayal, double crossing, romance.

The movie is set in the fictional kingdoms of Dasarana and Avanti, and has Krishna playing a double role.

One is Vikrama Simha, the Senapati of Dasarana, who defeats several Samantha Rajulu, and secures their allegiance to the King Kshemendra Bhupathi( Kantha Rao).

The other one is Prince Aditya Vardhana of Avanti, who is addicted to pleasures and debauchery, leading a carefree life.

Vikrama Simha’s bravery, sees the king grant him the sword of Bhavani, which however earns the envy of the Maha Mantri Bherunda( Prabhakar Reddy) who secretly covets the throne. In the meantime Princess Alakananda( Jayaprada) falls for Vikrama Simha. However Bherunda conspires to accuse Vikrama Simha of treason, forcing the King to exile him.

Aditya Vardhana on the other hand is infatuated with the court dancer Jaswanti( Radha), and is reprimanded by the Raja Mata( Waheeda Rehman). The wicked Raja Guru of Avanti( Satyanarayana) seeks to place his son on the throne, and is assisted by the Army commander Ugra Rahu( Giribabu) in his machinations.

Aditya Vardhana falls for a beautiful damsel Chandana( Mandakini) who is actually a Vishakanya. However when a guilt ridden Chandana attempts to end her life, Vikrama Simha saves her, and that is when he meets his look alike Aditya Vardhana who requests him to rule Avanthi in his place and resolve the crisis.

The movie shot at a budget of 4 Crore( quite high for that time), has quite rich production values and settings, especially the palace scenes, the arena. The action scenes are quite well shot too, especially the sword fights.

The movie does refer to ancient Indian history, Vikrama Simha is somewhat based on Gona Ganna Reddy, the loyal commander of the Kakatiya queen Rudrama Devi. The Vishakanya concept was based on a real life incident during the Mauryan era, when such a female was used to kill Chandragupta Maurya, but thwarted.

Vikrama Simha is the kind of role perfectly suited for Krishna and he pulls it off effortlessly, carrying the movie on his shoulders. His performance as Aditya Vardhana is decent.

Jayaprada looks gorgeous, a true princess, and performs well, while Mandakini does well in a femme fatale kinda role, and Radha provides the necessary glamour.

Waheeda Rehman appeared after a long time in Telugu cinema as the Raja Mata and does her part well. The villian trio of Satyanarayana, Prabhakar Reddy, Giri Babu do their parts well. Good support provided by Kanta Rao, Balayya, Gummadi, while Amjad Khan makes his debut in Telugu in a comic character.

Bappi Lahiri made his debut in Telugu, and the soundtrack became a super hit. The songs “Akasamlo Oka Tara”, “Waha Waah Yavannam”, “Idhi Kalayani Nenanukona” continue to be popular to date. Incidentally Krishna used a new singer Raj Sitaram, for all the songs, there was some dispute between him and SP Balasubramanyam then, though they later made up.

The movie was a success in Telugu, running for 50 days in 16 centres and 100 days in six centres, and grossing around 1.5 cr in it’s first week, a huge amount for those times.

However the Bollywood version Singhasan shot simultaneously starring Jeetendra turned out to be a flop, bringing in big losses for Krishna overall.

Krishna was once actor, who did bring in a Hollywood style narration into Telugu cinema, first cinemascope movie, first 70 mm movie, not to mention he was the one who bought in genres like spy, cowboy, thriller into Telugu cinema till then dominated mostly by Janapada, Pauranika or Social dramas. Simhasanam would remain one of his landmark movies both as an actor and director.
